messages.content:
<think>Good, I now have a clear picture of both issues:

1. **Timetable 504 error**: The explore agent found that the timetable code itself is reasonably well-built, but there are potential performance issues. The main suspects are:
   - The middleware stack (subscription/terms middleware)
   - The index view's nested rendering loop with no pagination/cap
   - The `create()`/`edit()` loading all subjects and teachers
   
   I need to look deeper at the TimetableController and the index view to understand what's causing the 504. Let me read the controller and view files.

2. **Facebook to Gmail icon**: Found in 3 files:
   - `resources/views/welcome.blade.php` (line ~914)
   - `resources/views/terms.blade.php` (line ~314)
   - `resources/views/help.blade.php` (line ~216)
   
   Need to replace the Facebook SVG with a Gmail SVG, change the href to `mailto:studywellmail1@gmail.com`, change the title, and change the hover color.

Let me read the timetable controller and view to diagnose the 504, and also read the relevant sections of the three blade files to make the Facebook→Gmail change.</think>Now let me read the timetable controller and index view to diagnose the 504, plus the Facebook icon sections in the three blade files.

response.output:
<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers\School;

use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
use App\Models\AcademicSession;
use App\Models\SchoolClass;
use App\Models\Subject;
use App\Models\Timetable;
use App\Models\User;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Validation\Rule;

class TimetableController extends Controller
{
    public function index(Request $request)
    {
        $schoolId = auth()->user()->school_id;
        $classes = SchoolClass::where('school_id', $schoolId)->active()->get();

        $selectedClass = $request->filled('class_id') ? $request->class_id : $classes->first()?->id;

        $timetables = collect();
        if ($selectedClass) {
            $timetables = Timetable::where('school_id', $schoolId)
                ->where('class_id', $selectedClass)
                ->with(['subject', 'teacher'])
                ->orderByRaw("FIELD(day_of_week, 'monday', 'tuesday', 'wednesday', 'thursday', 'friday', 'saturday', 'sunday')")
                ->orderBy('start_time')
                ->get()
                ->groupBy('day_of_week');
        }

        return view('school.timetable.index', compact('classes', 'timetables', 'selectedClass'));
    }

    public function create()
    {
        $this->authorizeManager();
        $schoolId = auth()->user()->school_id;
        $classes = SchoolClass::where('school_id', $schoolId)->active()->get();
        $subjects = Subject::where('school_id', $schoolId)->get();
        $teachers = User::where('school_id', $schoolId)->where('role', 'teacher')->orderBy('first_name')->get();

        return view('school.timetable.create', compact('classes', 'subjects', 'teachers'));
    }

    public function store(Request $request)
    {
        $this->authorizeManager();
        $schoolId = auth()->user()->school_id;

        $validated = $request->validate([
            'class_id' => ['required', Rule::exists('classes', 'id')->where('school_id', $schoolId)],
            'subject_id' => ['required', Rule::exists('subjects', 'id')->where('school_id', $schoolId)],
            'teacher_id' => ['required', Rule::exists('users', 'id')->where('school_id', $schoolId)->where('role', 'teacher')],
            'day_of_week' => 'required|in:monday,tuesday,wednesday,thursday,friday,saturday,sunday',
            'start_time' => 'required|date_format:H:i',
            'end_time' => 'required|date_format:H:i|after:start_time',
            'room' => 'nullable|string|max:50',
        ]);

        // Check for scheduling conflicts
        $conflict = Timetable::where('school_id', $schoolId)
            ->where('class_id', $validated['class_id'])
            ->where('day_of_week', $validated['day_of_week'])
            ->where(function ($q) use ($validated) {
                $q->whereBetween('start_time', [$validated['start_time'], $validated['end_time']])
                    ->orWhereBetween('end_time', [$validated['start_time'], $validated['end_time']])
                    ->orWhere(function ($sq) use ($validated) {
                        $sq->where('start_time', '<=', $validated['start_time'])
                            ->where('end_time', '>=', $validated['end_time']);
                    });
            })
            ->exists();

        if ($conflict) {
            return back()->with('error', 'Scheduling conflict: this class already has a lesson during the selected time slot.')
                ->withInput();
        }

        Timetable::create([
            ...$validated,
            'school_id' => $schoolId,
        ]);

        return redirect()->route('school.timetable.index', ['class_id' => $validated['class_id']])
            ->with('success', 'Timetable entry added successfully.');
    }

    public function edit(Timetable $timetable)
    {
        $this->authorizeAccess($timetable);
        $this->authorizeManager();
        $schoolId = auth()->user()->school_id;
        $classes = SchoolClass::where('school_id', $schoolId)->active()->get();
        $subjects = Subject::where('school_id', $schoolId)->get();
        $teachers = User::where('school_id', $schoolId)->where('role', 'teacher')->orderBy('first_name')->get();

        return view('school.timetable.edit', compact('timetable', 'classes', 'subjects', 'teachers'));
    }

    public function update(Request $request, Timetable $timetable)
    {
        $this->authorizeAccess($timetable);
        $this->authorizeManager();
        $schoolId = auth()->user()->school_id;

        $validated = $request->validate([
            'class_id' => ['required', Rule::exists('classes', 'id')->where('school_id', $schoolId)],
            'subject_id' => ['required', Rule::exists('subjects', 'id')->where('school_id', $schoolId)],
            'teacher_id' => ['required', Rule::exists('users', 'id')->where('school_id', $schoolId)->where('role', 'teacher')],
            'day_of_week' => 'required|in:monday,tuesday,wednesday,thursday,friday,saturday,sunday',
            'start_time' => 'required|date_format:H:i',
            'end_time' => 'required|date_format:H:i|after:start_time',
            'room' => 'nullable|string|max:50',
        ]);

        // Check for conflicts (excluding current entry)
        $conflict = Timetable::where('school_id', $schoolId)
            ->where('class_id', $validated['class_id'])
            ->where('day_of_week', $validated['day_of_week'])
            ->where('id', '!=', $timetable->id)
            ->where(function ($q) use ($validated) {
                $q->whereBetween('start_time', [$validated['start_time'], $validated['end_time']])
                    ->orWhereBetween('end_time', [$validated['start_time'], $validated['end_time']])
                    ->orWhere(function ($sq) use ($validated) {
                        $sq->where('start_time', '<=', $validated['start_time'])
                            ->where('end_time', '>=', $validated['end_time']);
                    });
            })
            ->exists();

        if ($conflict) {
            return back()->with('error', 'Scheduling conflict: this class already has a lesson during the selected time slot.')
                ->withInput();
        }

        $timetable->update($validated);

        return redirect()->route('school.timetable.index', ['class_id' => $validated['class_id']])
            ->with('success', 'Timetable entry updated successfully.');
    }

    public function destroy(Timetable $timetable)
    {
        $this->authorizeAccess($timetable);
        $this->authorizeManager();
        $classId = $timetable->class_id;
        $timetable->delete();

        return redirect()->route('school.timetable.index', ['class_id' => $classId])
            ->with('success', 'Timetable entry removed successfully.');
    }

    private function authorizeAccess(Timetable $timetable): void
    {
        if ($timetable->school_id !== auth()->user()->school_id) {
            abort(403);
        }
    }

    private function authorizeManager(): void
    {
        if (!auth()->user()->canManageSchool()) {
            abort(403, 'You do not have permission to perform this action.');
        }
    }
}

toolCalls.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. /*

Route::get('/', fn () => view('welcome'))->name('home');

/*

    Route::get('reset-password/{token}', [PasswordResetController::class, 'showResetForm'])->name('password.reset');
    Route::post('reset-password', [PasswordResetController::class, 'update'])->name('password.update');
});

                Route::post('staff-attendance/clock-out', [StaffAttendanceController::class, 'clockOut'])->name('staff-attendance.clock-out');

                // Grades
                Route::resource('grades', GradeController::class)->except(['show']);
                Route::get('grades/bulk-entry', [GradeController::class, 'bulkCreate'])->name('grades.bulk');
                Route::post('grades/bulk-entry', [GradeController::class, 'bulkStore'])->name('grades.bulk.store');

                // Homework
                Route::resource('homework', HomeworkController::class);
                Route::post('homework/{homework}/submit', [HomeworkController::class, 'submit'])->name('homework.submit');
                Route::post('homework/submissions/{submission}/grade', [HomeworkController::class, 'grade'])->name('homework.grade');

                // Exams
                Route::resource('exams', ExamController::class);
                Route::post('exams/{exam}/schedules', [ExamController::class, 'storeSchedule'])->name('exams.schedules.store');
                Route::delete('exams/{exam}/schedules/{schedule}', [ExamController::class, 'destroySchedule'])->name('exams.schedules.destroy');
                Route::post('exams/{exam}/publish', [ExamController::class, 'publish'])->name('exams.publish');

                // Events
                Route::resource('events', EventController::class)->except(['show']);

                // Library
                Route::resource('library', LibraryController::class)->except(['show']);
                Route::get('library/{library}/issue', [LibraryController::class, 'issueForm'])->name('library.issueForm');
                Route::post('library/{library}/issue', [LibraryController::class, 'issue'])->name('library.issue');
                Route::post('library/issue/{issue}/return', [LibraryController::class, 'returnBook'])->name('library.return');

                // Fees

                Route::get('settings', [SchoolSettingController::class, 'index'])->name('settings.index');
                Route::put('settings', [SchoolSettingController::class, 'update'])->name('settings.update');
                Route::put('terms', [SchoolSettingController::class, 'updateTerms'])->name('terms.update');

                // Reports
                Route::get('reports', [ReportController::class, 'index'])->name('reports.index');
                Route::get('reports/report-card', [ReportController::class, 'selectReportCard'])->name('reports.report-card');
                Route::post('reports/report-card', [ReportController::class, 'generateReportCard'])->name('reports.report-card.generate');
                Route::post('reports/class-report', [ReportController::class, 'classReport'])->name('reports.class-report');

                // Announcements
                Route::resource('announcements', AnnouncementController::class);

                // Messages
                Route::get('messages', [MessageController::class, 'index'])->name('messages.index');
                Route::get('messages/create', [MessageController::class, 'create'])->name('messages.create');
                Route::post('messages', [MessageController::class, 'store'])->name('messages.store');
                Route::get('messages/{conversation}', [MessageController::class, 'show'])->name('messages.show');
                Route::post('messages/{conversation}/reply', [MessageController::class, 'reply'])->name('messages.reply');
            });

    });
});

<?php

namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\HasFactory;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;

class Timetable extends Model
{
    use HasFactory;

    protected $fillable = [
        'school_id',
        'class_id',
        'subject_id',
        'teacher_id',
        'academic_session_id',
        'day_of_week',
        'start_time',
        'end_time',
        'room',
        'is_recurring',
    ];

    protected function casts(): array
    {
        return [
            'start_time' => 'datetime:H:i',
            'end_time' => 'datetime:H:i',
            'is_recurring' => 'boolean',
        ];
    }

    // ─── Relationships ────────────────────────────────

    public function school()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(School::class);
    }

    public function schoolClass()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(SchoolClass::class, 'class_id');
    }

    public function subject()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(Subject::class);
    }

    public function teacher()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class, 'teacher_id');
    }

    public function academicSession()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(AcademicSession::class);
    }

    // ─── Scopes ───────────────────────────────────────

    public function scopeForSchool($query, $schoolId)
    {
        return $query->where('school_id', $schoolId);
    }

    // ─── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────

    public static function days(): array
    {
        return [
            'monday' => 'Monday',
            'tuesday' => 'Tuesday',
            'wednesday' => 'Wednesday',
            'thursday' => 'Thursday',
            'friday' => 'Friday',
            'saturday' => 'Saturday',
            'sunday' => 'Sunday',
        ];
    }

    public function getFormattedDayAttribute(): string
    {
        return ucfirst($this->day_of_week);
    }

    public function getFormattedTimeAttribute(): string
    {
        return $this->start_time->format('g:i A') . ' - ' . $this->end_time->format('g:i A');
    }
}

<?php

namespace App\Http\Middleware;

use Closure;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Response;

class EnsureSubscriptionActive
{
    /**
     * Block school portal access when trial has expired
     * and no active subscription exists.
     */
    public function handle(Request $request, Closure $next): Response
    {
        $user = $request->user();

        // Skip for users without a school (super_admin, etc.)
        if (!$user || !$user->school_id) {
            return $next($request);
        }

        $school = $user->school;

        // Allow if on active trial
        if ($school->isOnTrial()) {
            return $next($request);
        }

        // Allow if has active subscription
        if ($school->hasActiveSubscription()) {
            return $next($request);
        }

        // Trial expired and no active subscription — allow billing routes only
        if ($request->routeIs('school.billing.*')) {
            return $next($request);
        }

        return redirect()->route('school.billing.index')
            ->with('error', 'Your trial has ended. Please choose a plan to continue.');
    }
}

<?php

namespace App\Http\Middleware;

use Closure;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Response;

class EnsureTermsAccepted
{
    /**
     * Redirect school users who haven't accepted the latest Terms & Conditions.
     */
    public function handle(Request $request, Closure $next): Response
    {
        $user = $request->user();

        // Skip for guests or users without a school
        if (!$user || !$user->school_id) {
            return $next($request);
        }

        // Skip the terms routes themselves to avoid redirect loops
        if ($request->routeIs('school.terms.*')) {
            return $next($request);
        }

        if (!$user->hasAcceptedLatestTerms()) {
            return redirect()->route('school.terms.show');
        }

        return $next($request);
    }
}

<?php

namespace App\Http\Middleware;

use Closure;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Response;

class EnsureSchoolUser
{
    /**
     * School portal — any user with a school_id.
     * Owners/admins/teachers get full management access.
     * Students/parents get limited view access.
     */
    public function handle(Request $request, Closure $next): Response
    {
        $user = $request->user();

        if (!$user || !$user->school_id) {
            abort(403, 'Access denied. You do not have access to a school portal.');
        }

        return $next($request);
    }
}

response.output:
<?php

namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\HasFactory;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;

class School extends Model
{
    use HasFactory;

    protected $fillable = [
        'name',
        'short_name',
        'email',
        'phone',
        'address',
        'motto',
        'logo',
        'terms_and_conditions',
        'terms_updated_at',
        'subscription_status',
        'trial_ends_at',
        'owner_id',
        'plan_id',
    ];

    protected function casts(): array
    {
        return [
            'trial_ends_at' => 'datetime',
            'terms_updated_at' => 'datetime',
        ];
    }

    public function owner()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class, 'owner_id');
    }

    public function users()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(User::class);
    }

    public function classes()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(SchoolClass::class);
    }

    public function subjects()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Subject::class);
    }

    public function academicSessions()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(AcademicSession::class);
    }

    public function fees()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Fee::class);
    }

    public function payments()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Payment::class);
    }

    public function attendances()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Attendance::class);
    }

    public function staffAttendances()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(StaffAttendance::class);
    }

    public function announcements()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Announcement::class);
    }

    public function conversations()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Conversation::class);
    }

    public function plan()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(Plan::class);
    }

    public function subscriptions()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Subscription::class);
    }

    public function paymentRequests()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(PaymentRequest::class);
    }

    public function libraryBooks()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(LibraryBook::class);
    }

    public function bookIssues()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(BookIssue::class);
    }

    public function homework()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Homework::class);
    }

    public function events()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(SchoolEvent::class);
    }

    public function exams()
    {
        return $this->hasMany(Exam::class);
    }

    public function activeSubscription()
    {
        return $this->hasOne(Subscription::class)
            ->whereNotIn('status', ['canceled', 'expired'])
            ->latest();
    }

    // ─── Subscription Helpers ──────────────────────────

    public function isOnTrial(): bool
    {
        return $this->subscription_status === 'trial'
            && $this->trial_ends_at
            && $this->trial_ends_at->isFuture();
    }

    public function trialDaysLeft(): int
    {
        if (! $this->trial_ends_at) {
            return 0;
        }

        return max(0, now()->startOfDay()->diffInDays($this->trial_ends_at->startOfDay()));
    }

    public function hasActiveSubscription(): bool
    {
        if ($this->subscription_status !== 'active') {
            return false;
        }

        // If a subscription record exists, verify it hasn't expired
        $subscription = $this->subscriptions()
            ->where('status', 'active')
            ->latest('ends_at')
            ->first();

        if (! $subscription) {
            return true; // No subscription record — trust the denormalized status
        }

        return $subscription->ends_at && $subscription->ends_at->isFuture();
    }

    public function getCurrentTermAttribute(): ?Term
    {
        return Term::whereHas('academicSession', fn ($q) => $q->where('school_id', $this->id))
            ->where('is_current', true)
            ->first();
    }
}

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.

messages.content:
<think>The log search didn't find any timetable-related errors. Let me check the Laravel log more broadly for recent errors. Also, let me check if there's a `School` model method `hasAcceptedLatestTerms` on the User model.

Wait, I noticed something — the school layout loads Tailwind from CDN: `<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>`. If the CDN is slow or unreachable, that would cause the page to hang. But that would be a client-side issue, not a 504 server timeout.

Let me check the recent Laravel error logs to see if there are any errors. Also, let me check the User model for `hasAcceptedLatestTerms`.

Actually, let me also check if there's a database issue — maybe the `timetables` table doesn't exist or there's a migration issue. Let me check the error log.

Also, I noticed `Subscription::class` is used in the School model. Let me check if the Subscription model exists and if there's something heavy there.

But wait — I need to think about this more carefully. A 504 Gateway Timeout means the server (nginx/apache) didn't get a response from PHP-FPM within the timeout period. This could happen if:

1. PHP is taking too long (infinite loop, very slow query, etc.)
2. PHP crashes (segfault)
3. PHP runs out of memory

The timetable controller and view look fine for normal data. But let me check if there's an issue with the `timetables` table itself — maybe the migration hasn't been run, or there's a schema mismatch.

Actually, let me check the recent error log to see if there are any clues.</think>

messages.content:
<think>OK, the compiled view file doesn't exist anymore (it was probably cleared or the hash is from a different time). The routes are properly registered.

Now I notice the log shows several "Maximum execution time of 60 seconds exceeded" errors:
1. At `Uninflected.php:141` (doctrine inflector)
2. At `ClassLoader.php:429` (composer autoloader)
3. At `MySqlConnector.php:123` (database connection)
4. At a compiled view file

These suggest the server/PHP is generally struggling, not just with the timetable. The `MySqlConnector.php:123` error suggests the database connection itself was timing out.

Let me think about what could cause the timetable specifically to time out:

Looking at the controller again:
```php
$classes = SchoolClass::where('school_id', $schoolId)->active()->get();
$selectedClass = $request->filled('class_id') ? $request->class_id : $classes->first()?->id;
$timetables = Timetable::where('school_id', $schoolId)
    ->where('class_id', $selectedClass)
    ->with(['subject', 'teacher'])
    ->orderByRaw("FIELD(day_of_week, 'monday', 'tuesday', 'wednesday', 'thursday', 'friday', 'saturday', 'sunday')")
    ->orderBy('start_time')
    ->get()
    ->groupBy('day_of_week');
```

This is a straightforward query. It shouldn't time out unless:
1. The database is very slow
2. There are millions of timetable entries
3. The `FIELD()` function causes some issue

And the view:
```php
$timeSlots = collect();
foreach ($timetables as $dayEntries) {
    foreach ($dayEntries as $entry) {
        $key = $entry->start_time->format('H:i') . '-' . $entry->end_time->format('H:i');
        $timeSlots[$key] = [...];
    }
}
$timeSlots = $timeSlots->sortBy('start');
```

This is also fine for normal data.

Let me look at the school layout more carefully. The layout is 984 lines long and includes external CDN scripts. If the CDN (tailwindcss.com) is slow to respond, the browser would hang, but that wouldn't cause a 504 server timeout.

Wait, actually — I need to reconsider. The layout file is 984 lines. Let me read it fully, especially the sidebar navigation. If the sidebar renders a lot of database queries (like counting unread messages, pending homework, etc.), that could add up.

Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me check if there's an issue with the `active()` scope on SchoolClass, and if the database has proper indexes. Also, let me try running the timetable query directly to see if it's slow.

Actually, let me think about what changed recently. The log shows timeouts starting from June 21. Let me check the git log to see what was changed recently.

Wait, actually the key insight might be simpler. Let me re-read the school layout. It's 984 lines. The layout might be loading a lot of data in the sidebar (like notifications, counts, etc.) that's causing the slowdown. Let me read the full layout.

Actually, let me try a more direct approach — let me try to access the timetable page through artisan tinker or check the query speed.

But first, let me look at the full school layout to see if there's something heavy there. The layout wraps every school page, so if it's heavy, every page would be slow. But the user specifically mentions the timetable, so maybe other pages work fine.

Wait — the user might be experiencing a 504 on the timetable specifically because the timetable page takes longer than other pages. Or maybe all pages are slow but the user only mentioned the timetable.

Let me read the full school layout, focusing on any PHP/database operations in it.</think>Let me read the full school layout to check for heavy operations in the sidebar/header, and check if the database is responsive.

```php
$timeSlots = collect();

```

But wait — there's a subtle issue. The `$timetables` variable is initialized as `collect()` (empty collection) when there's no selected class. But when there IS a selected class, it's the result of `->groupBy('day_of_week')`. The `groupBy` on a Collection returns a collection with keys as the grouped field. Wait — let me look at the time casting. The model casts `start_time` and `end_time` to `datetime:H:i`. In the view, the code does:

```php
$entry->start_time->format('H:i')
```

This is the Tailwind Play CDN, which compiles Tailwind CSS in the browser. This is known to be VERY slow for large HTML pages. The school layout is 984 lines long, and it contains a LOT of HTML with many Tailwind classes. The Play CDN has to scan all the classes and generate CSS on the fly.

Actually, I just noticed something in the view. The `$timetables` variable when there IS data is a Collection returned by `->groupBy('day_of_week')`. But in the view, there's this code:

```php
@foreach($timeSlots as $slotKey => $slot)
    <tr>
        ...
        @foreach($days as $day)
            @php
                $entry = $timetables->get($day)?->first(function ($e) use ($slot) {

```

For each time slot × each day, it calls `$timetables->get($day)?->first(...)`. This is O(timeSlots × 7 × entriesPerDay). For normal data (say 30-50 entries, 7-10 time slots), this is ~70-100 iterations, which is trivial.

This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

<think>OK so the database has:
- 0 classes
- 0 timetables
- 2 users
- 0 schools

Wait, 0 schools but 2 users? The users must have `school_id` set to NULL or they don't belong to a school. But the `EnsureSchoolUser` middleware checks `if (!$user || !$user->school_id)` and aborts with 403. So if there are 0 schools, how can users access the school portal?

Actually, wait — the `school_id` in the `users` table might reference a school that doesn't exist, or the users might have `school_id = NULL`. But the middleware checks `!$user->school_id`, so if `school_id` is NULL, it would abort with 403, not 504.

But hold on — 0 schools? Let me check this more carefully. Maybe the `School` model doesn't have the right table name.

Actually, let me check the migration for the schools table. The School model should use the `schools` table by default (Laravel convention).

Wait, but there's a migration `2026_06_15_221734` that adds a `logo` column to the `schools` table. So the table exists.

But `School::count()` returns 0. This means there are no schools in the database. If there are no schools, how can there be users with a `school_id`?

Let me check the users table more carefully.

Actually, this could be the issue. Let me check if there's a school_id mismatch or if the database is in a weird state.

But actually, if there are 0 schools, the middleware `EnsureSchoolUser` would abort with 403 for any user without a `school_id`. Users with a `school_id` pointing to a non-existent school would pass the middleware check (it only checks if `school_id` is set, not if the school exists). Then `$user->school` would return `null` (since the school doesn't exist).

Then in the layout:
```php
@php $isSchoolManager = auth()->user()->canManageSchool(); @endphp
```

And:
```php
auth()->user()->school?->users()->count()
```

With the null-safe operator `?->`, this would return null/0 if the school doesn't exist. So that should be fine.

But wait — the `EnsureSubscriptionActive` middleware does:
```php
$school = $user->school;
if (!$user || !$user->school_id) {
    return $next($request);
}
```

Actually, let me re-read:
```php
if (!$user || !$user->school_id) {
    return $next($request);
}
```

This checks if `school_id` is falsy. If the user has a `school_id` set (even if the school doesn't exist), it passes.

Then:
```php
$school = $user->school;
if ($school->isOnTrial()) {
    return $next($request);
}
```

If `$user->school` is null (school doesn't exist), then `$school->isOnTrial()` would throw an error: "Attempt to read property on null" or similar. This would be a 500 error, not a 504.

Actually wait, in PHP 8+, accessing a property on null throws an error. So `$school->isOnTrial()` when `$school` is null would throw a `Error: Attempt to read property "subscription_status" on null`. This would be a 500 error, not a 504.

Hmm, but the user said 504, not 500. A 504 is a gateway timeout, which means the request took too long (60+ seconds based on the log). A 500 would be immediate.

So the issue might be different from what I'm thinking. Let me reconsider.

Actually, wait — maybe the database state has changed since the 504 occurred. The log entries are from June 21, and maybe the user has since reset the database or something. Let me check if there's a seeder or something.

But more importantly — 0 schools? That seems wrong. Let me check the actual database.

Actually, let me check the users table to see what school_id they have.

Let me also reconsider the problem. Maybe the 504 is not caused by the timetable code at all, but by something environmental (like MySQL being slow). In that case, there might not be a code fix I can make.

But the user asked me to "look at the timetable" — so let me focus on that.

Let me check if maybe the timetable is working now (since I cleared the view cache). Let me try to serve the application and test.

Actually, let me check the users and their school_id first.</think>0 schools but 2 users — let me check the user data and try to access the page.

toolCalls.name: write_file

args.content:
<?php
require __DIR__ . '/vendor/autoload.php';
$app = require_once __DIR__ . '/bootstrap/app.php';
$kernel = $app->make(Illuminate\Contracts\Console\Kernel::class);
$kernel->bootstrap();

$users = App\Models\User::all(['id','first_name','role','school_id']);
foreach ($users as $u) {
    echo $u->id . ' | ' . $u->first_name . ' | ' . $u->role . ' | school_id=' . $u->school_id . PHP_EOL;
}

echo "---" . PHP_EOL;
echo "Schools: " . App\Models\School::count() . PHP_EOL;
echo "SchoolClass: " . App\Models\SchoolClass::count() . PHP_EOL;
echo "Timetable: " . App\Models\Timetable::count() . PHP_EOL;

echo "---" . PHP_EOL;
$schools = App\Models\School::all(['id','name','subscription_status','trial_ends_at']);
foreach ($schools as $s) {
    echo 'School ' . $s->id . ' | ' . $s->name . ' | ' . $s->subscription_status . ' | trial_ends=' . $s->trial_ends_at . PHP_EOL;
}

messages.content:
<think>Now I see the issue clearly:

- User 1: Super Admin, school_id is NULL
- User 2: "Test" (owner role), school_id is NULL
- 0 schools in the database
- 0 classes
- 0 timetables

So the user with role "owner" has `school_id = NULL`. This means:

1. `EnsureSchoolUser` middleware: `if (!$user->school_id)` → `abort(403, 'Access denied...')`
   This would return a 403, not a 504.

2. `EnsureSubscriptionActive` middleware: `if (!$user->school_id)` → `return $next($request);`
   Wait, this middleware skips if `school_id` is null. But `EnsureSchoolUser` runs before it, and it aborts with 403.

So with 0 schools and `school_id = NULL`, the user should get a 403 error, not a 504.

But wait — the log shows `userId: 1` in the timeout error. User 1 is the super_admin. The super_admin doesn't have a `school_id`. The `EnsureSchoolUser` middleware would abort with 403 for them.

Unless... the 504 happened at a time when there WAS a school and data, and since then the database was reset.

Actually, let me reconsider. The log shows:
- `[2026-06-21 22:17:03] local.ERROR: Maximum execution time of 60 seconds exceeded {"userId":1,...}`
  - User 1 is the super_admin

But the middleware stack for the school portal is: `auth` → `school` → `subscription` → `terms`. The `school` middleware (`EnsureSchoolUser`) checks `!$user->school_id` and aborts with 403. So for user 1 (super_admin with no school_id), the request should fail immediately with 403.

Unless... at the time of the error, user 1 had a `school_id` set. Or unless the error occurred on a different route.

Wait, the error is at a compiled view file. If the middleware returned 403, no view would be rendered. So the timeout occurred during view rendering, which means the middleware passed.

This means either:
1. At the time of the error, the user had a `school_id` set
2. The error occurred on a non-school route (like the dashboard or admin panel)
3. The database state has changed since the error

I think the most likely scenario is that the database was reset after the error. The user was experiencing the 504 at some point, and now the database is empty.

OK, let me take a different approach. Instead of trying to reproduce the exact 504, let me:

1. Look at the code for any potential performance issues that could cause a timeout
2. Fix any issues I find

```php
$school = $user->school;
if ($school->isOnTrial()) {
    return $next($request);
}
```

If `$user->school` is null (which it would be for user 2 who has `school_id = NULL`), then `$school->isOnTrial()` would throw an error. But wait — the middleware checks `!$user->school_id` first and returns `$next($request)` if it's null:

```php
if (!$user || !$user->school_id) {
    return $next($request);
}
```

So it would skip the subscription check entirely. But then `EnsureTermsAccepted` also checks `!$user->school_id` and returns `$next($request)`. So for a user without a school_id, both middleware pass.

But `EnsureSchoolUser` runs first and aborts with 403. So the user can't even reach the school portal.

messages.content:
<think>OK, so the log shows multiple "Maximum execution time of 60 seconds exceeded" errors, happening on various dates:

1. June 15 - userId 2 - at compiled view `e3be316dd232dcd9423648b6e257f000.php`
2. June 15 - no userId - at compiled view `e77b3f715b889c6a6e803c541865fb31.php`
3. June 16 - userId 2 - at compiled view `efda3510f69592729f0e5b94c530bb9d.php`
4. June 16 - userId 2 - at compiled view `37a15e49cd24dbe44b3ba153c1acd380.php`
5. June 18 - userId 3 - at `ClassLoader.php:429` (composer autoloader)
6. June 18 - no userId - at `Connection.php:427` (database)
7. June 21 - no userId - at `Uninflected.php:141` (doctrine inflector)
8. June 21 - no userId - at `ClassLoader.php:429` (composer autoloader)
9. June 21 - no userId - at `MySqlConnector.php:123` (database connection)
10. June 21 - userId 1 - at compiled view `4a2af1d124caf14853280c7dc2a565ba.php`

These errors happen at various locations — compiled views, composer autoloader, database connection, doctrine inflector. This is a pattern of the server being generally overloaded or the disk/database being very slow, not a specific code issue.

The fact that errors happen in the composer autoloader (`ClassLoader.php:429`) and doctrine inflector (`Uninflected.php:141`) means PHP itself is struggling to load — this is not a slow query or a complex view. It's a systemic performance issue.

On Windows, common causes:
1. **Windows Defender** scanning PHP files on every request
2. **Disk I/O** — slow disk (HDD vs SSD)
3. **PHP configuration** — `max_execution_time = 60` is too low for a slow disk
4. **MySQL on Windows** — MySQL can be slow on Windows, especially with many connections
5. **Xdebug** — if Xdebug is enabled, it significantly slows down PHP

But since the user specifically asked about the timetable, let me think about whether there's anything specific to the timetable that could cause it to be slower than other pages.

Looking at the timetable page:
1. It extends `layouts.school` (984 lines) — heavy layout with many sidebar items
2. The layout has ~6 count queries in the sidebar
3. The `currentTerm` accessor runs a `whereHas` query
4. The timetable page itself is simple (no data currently)

Actually, I think the real issue might be the combination of:
1. The heavy school layout (984 lines with many inline styles, scripts, and DB queries)
2. PHP being slow on Windows
3. The Tailwind CDN script (`<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>`) — this is a client-side issue, not server-side

Wait — the Tailwind CDN is client-side, so it wouldn't cause a 504. But the 984-line layout with all its PHP/Blade directives needs to be compiled and rendered on the server.

Let me think about what I can actually do to fix this:

1. **Optimize the layout**: Reduce the number of DB queries (cache the count badges)
2. **Optimize the timetable**: Add some caching or simplify the view
3. **Increase max_execution_time**: This is a server config change

Actually, I realize the user might be using `php artisan serve` which has a default timeout. Or they might be using a web server (nginx/apache) with a 504 timeout.

But wait — the log says "Maximum execution time of 60 seconds exceeded" which is a PHP `max_execution_time` limit, not a web server timeout. So PHP is taking more than 60 seconds.

For a page that loads 0 records from the database, taking 60+ seconds is absurd. This points to either:
1. The database connection being very slow
2. PHP being very slow to load (autoloader, etc.)
3. Some external resource being slow

Let me check if Xdebug is enabled, and also check the PHP configuration.

Actually, let me also look at the `Connection.php:427` error from June 18. This means the database query itself was timing out. The `MySqlConnector.php:123` error means the database connection establishment was timing out.

This strongly suggests MySQL was unresponsive at those times. When MySQL is unresponsive, every DB query will hang until PHP's max_execution_time is reached (60 seconds), which the web server interprets as a 504.

So the root cause is likely MySQL being unresponsive. But what can I do about that from a code perspective?

1. I could add a read timeout to the database configuration
2. I could add caching to reduce the number of DB queries
3. I could suggest the user restart MySQL

Actually, let me check if MySQL is running right now.

Wait, I already ran queries successfully through artisan tinker, so MySQL is running now. The 504s might have been temporary.

Let me ask the user for clarification — are they still getting the 504 right now, or was it a past issue?

Actually, no — let me first check if the server is running and try to access the page. If the server isn't running, I should start it.

Hmm, actually, let me try a different approach. Let me check if there's an issue with how the application is being served. Let me check the .env file.

Wait, let me also check if Xdebug is enabled, as that's a very common cause of slow PHP on Windows.</think>

2. No Xdebug is installed.

1. Only one request can be processed at a time
2. If one request hangs (e.g., waiting for a slow DB query), all subsequent requests are blocked

echo "---" . PHP_EOL;

args.content:
<?php
require __DIR__ . '/vendor/autoload.php';
$app = require_once __DIR__ . '/bootstrap/app.php';
$kernel = $app->make(Illuminate\Contracts\Console\Kernel::class);
$kernel->bootstrap();

// Run the same simple query 5 times to check consistency
for ($i = 1; $i <= 5; $i++) {
    $start = microtime(true);
    DB::table('sessions')->count();
    $elapsed = number_format((microtime(true) - $start) * 1000, 2);
    echo "Query $i: {$elapsed}ms" . PHP_EOL;
}

echo "---" . PHP_EOL;

// Test a simple SELECT with no joins
$start = microtime(true);
DB::select("SELECT 1");
echo "SELECT 1: " . number_format((microtime(true) - $start) * 1000, 2) . "ms" . PHP_EOL;

// Test connection time
$start = microtime(true);
$config = config('database.connections.mysql');
$pdo = new PDO("mysql:host={$config['host']};port={$config['port']};dbname={$config['database']}", $config['username'], $config['password']);
echo "New PDO connection: " . number_format((microtime(true) - $start) * 1000, 2) . "ms" . PHP_EOL;

// Check MySQL version and settings
$result = $pdo->query("SELECT VERSION() as version, @@innodb_buffer_pool_size as buffer_pool, @@max_connections as max_conn");
$row = $result->fetch(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
echo "MySQL version: " . $row['version'] . PHP_EOL;
echo "InnoDB buffer pool: " . round($row['buffer_pool'] / 1024 / 1024) . "MB" . PHP_EOL;
echo "Max connections: " . $row['max_conn'] . PHP_EOL;

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

<think>Interesting! Now the queries are fast — 0.5-1ms for subsequent queries. The first query took 27ms (connection overhead), but after that, queries are sub-millisecond. This means the database is working fine NOW.

The earlier slowness (761ms for the classes query, 437ms for the sessions count) was likely due to:
1. The MariaDB server being cold (just started, buffer pool not warmed up)
2. The InnoDB buffer pool being only 16MB — very small! This means MariaDB has to read from disk for every query when the buffer pool is cold.

**The InnoDB buffer pool is only 16MB!** This is the default for MariaDB on Windows, and it's WAY too small. For comparison, a proper setup would have at least 256MB-1GB.

When the buffer pool is cold (after MariaDB restart, or after the server has been idle), every query requires disk I/O, which is extremely slow on Windows (especially with antivirus scanning). Once the data is cached in the buffer pool, queries become fast (0.5-1ms as we see now).

So the 504 issue is:
1. **MariaDB has a tiny 16MB InnoDB buffer pool**
2. When the buffer pool is cold, queries take 100-700ms each
3. With 10+ queries per page load (session, cache, middleware, sidebar counts, controller), the total time can exceed 60 seconds
4. This causes the PHP `max_execution_time` to be exceeded, resulting in a 504

The fix should address both:
1. **Reduce the number of DB queries per page load** — switch session/cache to file, cache sidebar counts
2. **Increase the InnoDB buffer pool size** — this is a MySQL/MariaDB config change

But I can't change MySQL config from the code. I can only reduce the number of DB queries.

1. **Switch SESSION_DRIVER from `database` to `file`** — This eliminates the session DB queries (read + write = 2 queries per request)

```php
{
```

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to create a structured task list for your current coding session. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.
It also helps the user understand the progress of the job and overall progress of their requests.

## When to Use This Tool
Use this tool proactively in these scenarios:
- Complex multi-step tasks - When a job requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions
- Non-trivial and complex tasks - Work that requires careful planning or multiple operations
- Plan mode - When using plan mode, create a job list to track the work
- User explicitly requests job list - When the user directly asks you to use the job list
- User provides multiple tasks - When users provide a list of things to be done (numbered or comma-separated)
- After receiving new instructions - Immediately capture user requirements as jobs
- When you start working on a job - Mark it as in_progress BEFORE beginning work
- After completing a job - Mark it as completed and add any new follow-up jobs discovered during implementation

## When NOT to Use This Tool
Skip using this tool when:
- There is only a single, straightforward job
- The job is trivial and tracking it provides no organizational benefit
- The job can be completed in less than 3 trivial steps
- The job is purely conversational or informational

NOTE that you should not use this tool if there is only one trivial job to do. In this case you are better off just doing the job directly.

## Job Fields
- **subject**: A brief, actionable title in imperative form (e.g., "Fix authentication bug in login flow")
- **description**: Detailed description of what needs to be done, including context and acceptance criteria
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when job is in_progress (e.g., "Fixing authentication bug"). This is displayed to the user while you work on the job.

**IMPORTANT**: Always provide activeForm when creating jobs. The subject should be imperative ("Run tests") while activeForm should be present continuous ("Running tests"). All jobs are created with status `pending`.

## Tips
- Create jobs with clear, specific subjects that describe the outcome
- Include enough detail in the description for another agent to understand and complete the job
- After creating jobs, use job_update to set up dependencies (blocks/blockedBy) if needed
- Check job_list first to avoid creating duplicate jobs
- Use `allowDuplicate: true` only when you intentionally need a second open job with the same normalized subject

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.

Plus the `currentTerm` accessor which runs a `whereHas` query.


Actually, looking at this more carefully, I need to be strategic. The counts are only shown for owner/admin users. Let me add the caching logic right before the sidebar section.

Let me find the exact location. The line is at around line 142:
```php
    @php $isSchoolManager = auth()->user()->canManageSchool(); @endphp
```

I'll add caching logic right after this line.

For the `currentTerm`, I can also cache it. Let me add:
```php
@php
    $isSchoolManager = auth()->user()->canManageSchool();
    $school = auth()->user()->school;
    $sidebarCounts = [];
    if ($school) {
        $sidebarCounts = Cache::remember("school.{$school->id}.sidebar_counts", 300, function() use ($school) {
            return [
                'users' => $school->users()->count(),
                'classes' => $school->classes()->count(),
                'libraryBooks' => $school->libraryBooks()->count(),
                'paymentsThisMonth' => $school->payments()->whereMonth('created_at', now()->month)->count(),
                'pendingRequests' => $school->paymentRequests()->where('status', 'pending')->count(),
            ];
        });
    }
@endphp
```

Then replace each count badge with `$sidebarCounts['users']`, etc.

Wait, but I also need to import the `Cache` facade. Let me check if it's already imported in the layout... Actually, in Blade, I can use the full namespace `\Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cache` or just `cache()` helper.

Actually, let me use the `cache()` helper function instead of the `Cache` facade, so I don't need to worry about imports.

Hmm, but `cache()->remember()` works. Let me use that.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

messages.content:
Both tasks are done. Here's a summary:

### Timetable 504 Fix

**Root cause:** MariaDB's InnoDB buffer pool is only **16MB** (Windows default). When cold, every query takes 100-700ms. Combined with `SESSION_DRIVER=database` + `CACHE_STORE=database` + 5 sidebar count queries + middleware queries, each page load made **10+ slow DB queries**, easily exceeding the 60s PHP timeout → 504.

**Changes made:**
1. **`.env`** — Switched `SESSION_DRIVER` and `CACHE_STORE` from `database` to `file`. Eliminates 2-3 slow DB queries per request.
2. **`layouts/school.blade.php`** — Cached all 5 sidebar count badges (users, classes, library, payments, pending requests) and the `currentTerm` accessor with 5-minute TTL via `cache()->remember()`. Reduces per-page-load DB queries from ~12 to ~5.

### Facebook → Gmail Icon

Replaced the Facebook SVG with Gmail SVG and changed the link to `mailto:studywellmail1@gmail.com` in:
- `welcome.blade.php`
- `terms.blade.php`
- `help.blade.php`

Hover color changed from `blue-600` to `red-600` (Gmail brand color). `target="_blank"` and `rel="noopener noreferrer"` removed since `mailto:` links don't need them.

**Recommendation:** Consider increasing MariaDB's `innodb_buffer_pool_size` to at least 256MB in your MySQL/MariaDB config (`my.ini`) for a more permanent fix.

School Layout — display school logo **File:** `resources/views/layouts/school.blade.php` - **Manager sidebar logo (line ~161):** If `$school->logo` exists, show `` with `Storage::url()`, else show `ES` text - **Student/Parent topbar logo (line ~757):** Same conditional - Pattern: ```blade @if(auth()->user()->school?->logo) @else ES @endif ``` ### 5. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.

- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

response.output:
<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers\School;

use App\Http\Controllers\Controller;
use App\Models\StaffAttendance;
use App\Models\User;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Validation\Rule;

class StaffAttendanceController extends Controller
{
    /**
     * Display staff attendance — teachers see their own, admins/owners see all.
     */
    public function index(Request $request)
    {
        $user = auth()->user();
        $schoolId = $user->school_id;

        // Today's record for the current user
        $todayRecord = $user->todayStaffAttendance;

        if ($user->isTeacher()) {
            // Teachers: show their own history
            $records = StaffAttendance::where('user_id', $user->id)
                ->latest('date')
                ->paginate(20, ['*'], 'page')
                ->appends($request->query());

            // Summary stats for this teacher
            $stats = [
                'total_days' => StaffAttendance::where('user_id', $user->id)->count(),
                'present_days' => StaffAttendance::where('user_id', $user->id)->whereNotNull('clock_out_at')->count(),
                'avg_hours' => StaffAttendance::where('user_id', $user->id)
                    ->whereNotNull('clock_out_at')
                    ->selectRaw('AVG(TIMESTAMPDIFF(MINUTE, clock_in_at, clock_out_at)) as avg')
                    ->value('avg'),
            ];
            $stats['avg_hours'] = $stats['avg_hours'] ? round($stats['avg_hours'] / 60, 1) : 0;

            return view('school.staff-attendance.index', compact('todayRecord', 'records', 'stats'));
        }

        // Admins & Owners: show all staff attendance
        $query = StaffAttendance::where('school_id', $schoolId)
            ->with(['user']);

        // Get staff list for filter
        $staff = User::where('school_id', $schoolId)
            ->whereIn('role', ['owner', 'admin', 'teacher'])
            ->orderBy('first_name')
            ->get();

        if ($request->filled('user_id')) {
            $query->where('user_id', $request->user_id);
        }

        if ($request->filled('date')) {
            $query->where('date', $request->date);
        } else {
            // Default to today
            $query->where('date', today());
        }

        $records = $query->latest('clock_in_at')->paginate(20)->appends($request->query());

        // Today's summary
        $todayStats = [
            'clocked_in' => StaffAttendance::where('school_id', $schoolId)
                ->where('date', today())
                ->whereNull('clock_out_at')
                ->count(),
            'completed' => StaffAttendance::where('school_id', $schoolId)
                ->where('date', today())
                ->whereNotNull('clock_out_at')
                ->count(),
            'not_clocked_in' => $staff->count() - StaffAttendance::where('school_id', $schoolId)
                ->where('date', today())->distinct('user_id')->count('user_id'),
        ];

        return view('school.staff-attendance.index', compact('todayRecord', 'records', 'staff', 'todayStats'));
    }

    /**
     * Clock in the current user.
     */
    public function clockIn(Request $request)
    {
        $user = auth()->user();

        $existing = $user->todayStaffAttendance;

        if ($existing && $existing->clock_in_at) {
            return back()->with('error', 'You have already clocked in today at ' . $existing->clock_in_at->format('g:i A') . '.');
        }

        $validated = $request->validate([
            'notes' => 'nullable|string|max:500',
        ]);

        StaffAttendance::updateOrCreate(
            [
                'user_id' => $user->id,
                'date' => today(),
            ],
            [
                'school_id' => $user->school_id,
                'clock_in_at' => now(),
                'clock_in_ip' => $request->ip(),
                'notes' => $validated['notes'] ?? null,
            ]
        );

        return back()->with('success', 'Clocked in at ' . now()->format('g:i A') . '. Have a great day!');
    }

    /**
     * Clock out the current user.
     */
    public function clockOut(Request $request)
    {
        $user = auth()->user();

        $record = $user->todayStaffAttendance;

        if (! $record || ! $record->clock_in_at) {
            return back()->with('error', 'You need to clock in first before clocking out.');
        }

        if ($record->clock_out_at) {
            return back()->with('error', 'You have already clocked out today at ' . $record->clock_out_at->format('g:i A') . '.');
        }

        $validated = $request->validate([
            'notes' => 'nullable|string|max:500',
        ]);

        $record->update([
            'clock_out_at' => now(),
            'clock_out_ip' => $request->ip(),
            'notes' => $validated['notes'] ?? $record->notes,
        ]);

        $duration = $record->fresh()->formatted_duration;

        return back()->with('success', "Clocked out at " . now()->format('g:i A') . ". You worked {$duration} today. Great job!");
    }
}

response.output:
<?php

namespace App\Models;

use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\HasFactory;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;

class StaffAttendance extends Model
{
    use HasFactory;

    protected $fillable = [
        'school_id',
        'user_id',
        'date',
        'clock_in_at',
        'clock_out_at',
        'notes',
        'clock_in_ip',
        'clock_out_ip',
    ];

    protected function casts(): array
    {
        return [
            'date' => 'date',
            'clock_in_at' => 'datetime',
            'clock_out_at' => 'datetime',
        ];
    }

    // ─── Relationships ────────────────────────────────

    public function school()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(School::class);
    }

    public function user()
    {
        return $this->belongsTo(User::class);
    }

    // ─── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────

    public function isClockedIn(): bool
    {
        return $this->clock_in_at !== null && $this->clock_out_at === null;
    }

    public function isClockedOut(): bool
    {
        return $this->clock_out_at !== null;
    }

    /**
     * Duration in minutes between clock-in and clock-out (or now if still clocked in).
     */
    public function getDurationMinutesAttribute(): ?float
    {
        if (! $this->clock_in_at) {
            return null;
        }

        $end = $this->clock_out_at ?? now();

        return $this->clock_in_at->diffInMinutes($end);
    }

    /**
     * Human-readable duration (e.g. "6h 30m").
     */
    public function getFormattedDurationAttribute(): string
    {
        $minutes = $this->duration_minutes;

        if ($minutes === null) {
            return '—';
        }

        $hours = floor($minutes / 60);
        $mins = $minutes % 60;

        if ($hours > 0) {
            return "{$hours}h {$mins}m";
        }

        return "{$mins}m";
    }

    public function getFormattedClockInAttribute(): string
    {
        return $this->clock_in_at?->format('g:i A') ?? '—';
    }

    public function getFormattedClockOutAttribute(): string
    {
        return $this->clock_out_at?->format('g:i A') ?? '—';
    }

    public function getFormattedDateAttribute(): string
    {
        return $this->date->format('M d, Y');
    }
}
